events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: Locates & marking, misreading mark colors. Colors given: red=electric, yellow=gas, blue=water, green=sewer, orange=comm. The observed field condition is misreading mark colors. Need decide compliance per 811/CGA best practices. CGA color code standard: Red = electric, Yellow = gas/oil/steam, Orange = communication/telecom, Blue = potable water, Green = sewer/drainage. The scenario lists red=electric, yellow=gas, blue=water, green=sewer, orange=comm - pure ID item. Wait "misreading mark colors (red=electric, yellow=gas, blue=water, green=sewer, orange=comm - pure ID item)" The parenthetical seems to state correct colors? It says misreading mark colors, then lists correct mapping? Maybe the observed condition is that workers are misreading colors, e.g., they think red=electric etc? Actually the list matches CGA. But phrase "misreading mark colors" indicates non-compliance. Need determine fail. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Misreading utility locate colors can cause strikes to electric/gas lines, catastrophic. Critical maybe. Need produce JSON with required fields. percent_complete maybe 80? value 0. sound_source empty? Use "" maybe. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings: locate marks misread, incorrect color code interpretation. actions: retrain personnel, verify locate marks against CGA color code, re-mark as needed, hold work until corrected. references: CGA Best Practices, 811 Dig Safe. Need ensure no prose outside JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Personnel are misreading utility locate mark colors, violating the standardized color code.",
    "Incorrect color interpretation creates high risk of striking electric, gas, or other critical buried utilities."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop excavation/digging work in affected area until locate color interpretation is corrected.",
    "Retrain all relevant personnel on CGA/811 standardized color code for utility locates.",
    "Re-verify all existing locate marks against the correct color code and re-mark as needed.",
    "Have a competent person inspect and sign off before resuming work."
  ],
  "rationale": "811/CGA best practices require standardized utility locate mark colors to be read and applied correctly. Misreading these colors is a direct non-compliance and creates an imminent hazard of utility strikes, so the work fails inspection.",
  "references": [
    "CGA Best Practices",
    "811 Dig Safe / One Call color code standards"
  ]
}
